protocol_definition.name: Dynamic Task Allocation
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents negotiate roles, execute an allocation, adapt to gaps, and Writer finalizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Agents see shared negotiation history and relevant intermediate work.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after adaptation and Writer output or when the interaction budget is reached.

intermediate_messages.content:
**Deliverables:**
- System Overview
- Main Components and Responsibilities
- End-to-End Processing Workflow
- REST API Design
- Data Model
- Suggested Project Structure
- Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Testing Plan
- Design Tradeoff

**Hard Constraints:**
- No external tools are allowed.
- Design should focus on backend architecture for document-processing and search service.
- Design should support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of duplicate records, and easy addition of new document formats.
- Maintainability for a small development team is a key requirement.

**Factual Checks:**
- Ensure the design covers all required components and responsibilities.
- Verify that the design supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown).
- Confirm that the design includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies.
- Ensure the testing plan covers various scenarios and edge cases.

**Execution Plan:**
1. **System Overview:**
   - Provide a high-level description of the document-processing and search service architecture.

2. **Main Components and Responsibilities:**
   - Define the key components of the system and their responsibilities.

3. **End-to-End Processing Workflow:**
   - Outline the step-by-step workflow from file upload to document search.

4. **REST API Design:**
   - Design the API endpoints, request data, and response data for interacting with the service.

5. **Data Model:**
   - Define the data structures and relationships needed to store files, metadata, text, and searchable sections.

6. **Suggested Project Structure:**
   - Propose a project structure that facilitates maintainability and easy addition of new document formats.

7. **Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention:**
   - Detail how errors will be handled, retries managed, and duplicate records prevented.

8. **Testing Plan:**
   - Develop a plan to test the system thoroughly, covering different scenarios and edge cases.

9. **Design Tradeoff:**
   - Identify and explain at least one meaningful design tradeoff made in the architecture.

Ensure each section is well-documented, clear, and aligns with the requirements specified in the prompt.

I will take responsibility for designing the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service. I will ensure that the proposed design covers all the required components and responsibilities, supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown), includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies, and covers various testing scenarios and edge cases.

I will start by outlining the system overview, defining the main components and their responsibilities, detailing the end-to-end processing workflow, designing the REST API, defining the data model, proposing a project structure, detailing error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention strategies, creating a testing plan, and identifying at least one meaningful design tradeoff in the architecture.

I will ensure that the design is well-documented, clear, and aligns with the requirements specified in the prompt.

I will take responsibility for designing the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service. I will ensure that the proposed design covers all the required components and responsibilities, supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown), includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies, and covers various testing scenarios and edge cases.

I will start by outlining the system overview, defining the main components and their responsibilities, detailing the end-to-end processing workflow, designing the REST API, defining the data model, proposing a project structure, detailing error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention strategies, creating a testing plan, and identifying at least one meaningful design tradeoff in the architecture.

I will ensure that the design is well-documented, clear, and aligns with the requirements specified in the prompt.

I will verify the proposed design for the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service. I will ensure that the design covers all required components and responsibilities, supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown), includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies, and covers various testing scenarios and edge cases.

I will independently verify each section of the design, including the system overview, main components and responsibilities, end-to-end processing workflow, REST API design, data model, suggested project structure, error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention, testing plan, and design tradeoff.

**Allocation:**

As the Manager, I assign the task of designing the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service to the Researcher. The Researcher will be responsible for ensuring that the proposed design covers all required components and responsibilities, supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown), includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies, and covers various testing scenarios and edge cases.

The Researcher will start by outlining the system overview, defining the main components and their responsibilities, detailing the end-to-end processing workflow, designing the REST API, defining the data model, proposing a project structure, detailing error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention strategies, creating a testing plan, and identifying at least one meaningful design tradeoff in the architecture.

Upon completion, the Critic will verify the proposed design independently, ensuring that each section aligns with the requirements specified in the prompt and that all hard constraints and factual checks are met.

The Researcher should provide evidence and constraints checks for each section before proceeding to the next, ensuring that all hard requirements are verified before final approval.

  "type": "markdown",

  "type": "markdown",
  "sections": [
    "System Overview",
    "Main Components and Responsibilities",
    "End-to-End Processing Workflow",
    "REST API Design",
    "Data Model",
    "Suggested Project Structure",
    "Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention",
    "Testing Plan",
    "Design Tradeoff"
  ]
}

**System Overview:**

The backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service will consist of components for file upload, validation, text extraction, indexing, search functionality, and status reporting. Asynchronous processing will be a key feature to handle large files efficiently. The system will ensure maintainability for a small development team by employing clear separation of concerns and modular design.

**Main Components and Responsibilities:**

1. File Upload Component:
   - Responsible for accepting file uploads from users.
   - Validates file type and size.
   - Stores original files and metadata.

2. Text Extraction Component:
   - Extracts text content from uploaded files.
   - Divides text into searchable sections.

3. Indexing Component:
   - Creates a searchable index from the extracted text.
   - Supports efficient document search functionality.

4. Status Reporting Component:
   - Reports processing status to users.
   - Handles asynchronous processing and retryable failed steps.

**End-to-End Processing Workflow:**

1. User uploads a file.
2. File Upload Component validates and stores the file.
3. Text Extraction Component extracts text and divides it into sections.
4. Indexing Component creates a searchable index.
5. Status Reporting Component updates the user on processing status.
6. Users can search for documents based on indexed content.

**REST API Design:**

- **POST /upload**: Upload a file.
  - Request Data: File data.
  - Response Data: Upload status.

- **GET /search**: Search for documents.
  - Request Data: Search query.
  - Response Data: Search results.

**Data Model:**

- **File**: id, name, type, size, content.
- **Document**: id, file_id, title, content.
- **Index**: id, document_id, keyword.

**Suggested Project Structure:**

- **controllers/**: FileUploadController, SearchController.
- **services/**: FileUploadService, TextExtractionService, IndexingService.
- **models/**: FileModel, DocumentModel, IndexModel.
- **utils/**: ErrorHandlingUtil, RetryUtil.

**Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention:**

- Implement robust error handling at each component level.
- Retry failed steps with exponential backoff.
- Prevent duplicate records by checking existing file metadata before processing.

**Testing Plan:**

- Unit tests for each component.
- Integration tests for end-to-end workflow.
- Test edge cases like large file uploads, invalid file types, and concurrent requests.

**Design Tradeoff:**

One design tradeoff could be between processing speed and resource utilization. Opting for faster text extraction and indexing algorithms may require more computational resources, impacting scalability. Balancing speed with resource efficiency will be crucial for optimal system performance.

Ensure all components are well-documented, align with the requirements, and are ready for verification by the Critic.

I will now proceed to verify the proposed design for the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service. I will independently verify each section of the design, ensuring that all hard constraints and factual checks are met before final approval. I will provide evidence and constraints checks for each section to ensure alignment with the requirements specified in the prompt.

    "REST API Design",
    "Data Model",
    "Suggested Project Structure",
    "Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention",
    "Testing Plan",
    "Design Tradeoff"
  ]
}

messages.content: You are Planner. Decompose the task into concrete deliverables, hard constraints, factual checks, and a concise execution plan. Do not invent schedules, future work, or external evidence.

You are acting as role: Planner.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Available Tools:
[]

Tool Expectations:
{}

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

Produce a software design covering: system overview; main components and responsibilities; end-to-end workflow; REST API design; data model; suggested project structure; error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention; testing plan; and at least one meaningful design tradeoff.

Include a Component Table with columns: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output. Include an API Table with columns: Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, example JSON, or code snippets may be included when they clarify the design.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.

Required Output Format:
{
  "type": "markdown",
  "sections": [
    "System Overview",
    "Main Components and Responsibilities",
    "End-to-End Processing Workflow",
    "REST API Design",
    "Data Model",
    "Suggested Project Structure",
    "Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention",
    "Testing Plan",
    "Design Tradeoff"
  ]
}

Visible prior context:
Negotiation starts without a fixed allocation.

Instruction:
Propose an allocation for work that can be completed in this run. Volunteer for responsibilities, identify dependencies and verification checks, and do not invent future schedules.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.

You are Researcher. Gather and organize evidence available through the permitted tools and prompt. Reject irrelevant results, preserve exact source identifiers, distinguish verified facts from assumptions, and respect the task tool requirement.

You are acting as role: Researcher.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Available Tools:
[]

Tool Expectations:
{}

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

Produce a software design covering: system overview; main components and responsibilities; end-to-end workflow; REST API design; data model; suggested project structure; error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention; testing plan; and at least one meaningful design tradeoff.

Include a Component Table with columns: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output. Include an API Table with columns: Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, example JSON, or code snippets may be included when they clarify the design.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.

Required Output Format:
{
  "type": "markdown",
  "sections": [
    "System Overview",
    "Main Components and Responsibilities",
    "End-to-End Processing Workflow",
    "REST API Design",
    "Data Model",
    "Suggested Project Structure",
    "Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention",
    "Testing Plan",
    "Design Tradeoff"
  ]
}

Visible prior context:
[Negotiation]
Negotiation starts without a fixed allocation.

[Planner allocation proposal]
**Deliverables:**
- System Overview
- Main Components and Responsibilities
- End-to-End Processing Workflow
- REST API Design
- Data Model
- Suggested Project Structure
- Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
- Testing Plan
- Design Tradeoff

**Hard Constraints:**
- No external tools are allowed.
- Design should focus on backend architecture for document-processing and search service.
- Design should support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of duplicate records, and easy addition of new document formats.
- Maintainability for a small development team is a key requirement.

**Factual Checks:**
- Ensure the design covers all required components and responsibilities.
- Verify that the design supports the specified file types (PDF, TXT, Markdown).
- Confirm that the design includes error handling, retry mechanisms, and duplicate prevention strategies.
- Ensure the testing plan covers various scenarios and edge cases.

**Execution Plan:**
1. **System Overview:**
   - Provide a high-level description of the document-processing and search service architecture.

2. **Main Components and Responsibilities:**
   - Define the key components of the system and their responsibilities.

3. **End-to-End Processing Workflow:**
   - Outline the step-by-step workflow from file upload to document search.

4. **REST API Design:**
   - Design the API endpoints, request data, and response data for interacting with the service.

5. **Data Model:**
   - Define the data structures and relationships needed to store files, metadata, text, and searchable sections.

6. **Suggested Project Structure:**
   - Propose a project structure that facilitates maintainability and easy addition of new document formats.

7. **Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention:**
   - Detail how errors will be handled, retries managed, and duplicate records prevented.

8. **Testing Plan:**
   - Develop a plan to test the system thoroughly, covering different scenarios and edge cases.

9. **Design Tradeoff:**
   - Identify and explain at least one meaningful design tradeoff made in the architecture.

Ensure each section is well-documented, clear, and aligns with the requirements specified in the prompt.

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

Produce a software design covering: system overview; main components and responsibilities; end-to-end workflow; REST API design; data model; suggested project structure; error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention; testing plan; and at least one meaningful design tradeoff.

Include a Component Table with columns: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output. Include an API Table with columns: Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, example JSON, or code snippets may be included when they clarify the design.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.

[Analyst execution]
**System Overview:**

The backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service will consist of components for file upload, validation, text extraction, indexing, search functionality, and status reporting. Asynchronous processing will be a key feature to handle large files efficiently. The system will ensure maintainability for a small development team by employing clear separation of concerns and modular design.

**Main Components and Responsibilities:**

1. File Upload Component:
   - Responsible for accepting file uploads from users.
   - Validates file type and size.
   - Stores original files and metadata.

2. Text Extraction Component:
   - Extracts text content from uploaded files.
   - Divides text into searchable sections.

3. Indexing Component:
   - Creates a searchable index from the extracted text.
   - Supports efficient document search functionality.

4. Status Reporting Component:
   - Reports processing status to users.
   - Handles asynchronous processing and retryable failed steps.

**End-to-End Processing Workflow:**

1. User uploads a file.
2. File Upload Component validates and stores the file.
3. Text Extraction Component extracts text and divides it into sections.
4. Indexing Component creates a searchable index.
5. Status Reporting Component updates the user on processing status.
6. Users can search for documents based on indexed content.

**REST API Design:**

- **POST /upload**: Upload a file.
  - Request Data: File data.
  - Response Data: Upload status.

- **GET /search**: Search for documents.
  - Request Data: Search query.
  - Response Data: Search results.

**Data Model:**

- **File**: id, name, type, size, content.
- **Document**: id, file_id, title, content.
- **Index**: id, document_id, keyword.

**Suggested Project Structure:**

- **controllers/**: FileUploadController, SearchController.
- **services/**: FileUploadService, TextExtractionService, IndexingService.
- **models/**: FileModel, DocumentModel, IndexModel.
- **utils/**: ErrorHandlingUtil, RetryUtil.

**Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention:**

- Implement robust error handling at each component level.
- Retry failed steps with exponential backoff.
- Prevent duplicate records by checking existing file metadata before processing.

**Testing Plan:**

- Unit tests for each component.
- Integration tests for end-to-end workflow.
- Test edge cases like large file uploads, invalid file types, and concurrent requests.

**Design Tradeoff:**

One design tradeoff could be between processing speed and resource utilization. Opting for faster text extraction and indexing algorithms may require more computational resources, impacting scalability. Balancing speed with resource efficiency will be crucial for optimal system performance.

Ensure all components are well-documented, align with the requirements, and are ready for verification by the Critic.

[Critic execution]
I will now proceed to verify the proposed design for the backend software architecture for the document-processing and search service. I will independently verify each section of the design, ensuring that all hard constraints and factual checks are met before final approval. I will provide evidence and constraints checks for each section to ensure alignment with the requirements specified in the prompt.

Resolve conflicts by checking the task constraints, and do not repeat unsupported consensus claims.
